results.title: Eevee - Wikipedia
results.content:
Eevee is a species of fictional creatures called Pokémon created for the Pokémon media franchise. Developed by Game Freak and published by Nintendo, the Japanese franchise began in 1996 with the video games Pokémon Red and Green for the Game Boy, which were later released in North America as Pokémon Red and Blue in 1998. In these games and their sequels, the player assumes the role of a Trainer whose goal is to capture and use the creatures' special abilities to combat other Pokémon. Some Pokémon can transform into stronger species through a process called evolution via various means, such as exposure to specific items. Each Pokémon has one or two elemental types, which define its advantages and disadvantages when battling other Pokémon. A major goal in each game is to complete the [...] Wikipedia

# Eevee

Eevee (/ˈiːvi/ ⓘ), known in Japan as Eievui (Japanese: イーブイ, Hepburn: Ībui), is a Pokémon species in the Pokémon franchise. Created by Satoshi Tajiri and designed by Motofumi Fujiwara, it first appeared in the video games Pokémon Red and Blue. It later appeared in a variety of merchandise, spin-off titles, and both animated and printed adaptations of the franchise. It is also the game mascot and starter Pokémon for Pokémon: Let's Go, Eevee! [...] According to the Pokémon video games, Eevee is a mammalian creature with brown fur, a bushy tail that has a cream-colored tip, and a furry collar that is also cream-colored. Eevee has brown eyes, big ears, and pink paw pads. Eevee is said to have an irregularly shaped genetic structure, enabling it to evolve into multiple Pokémon. Fujiwara stated of Eevee "I wanted to create a blank slate Pokémon". Eevee's design draws upon his vague childhood memories, including an instance where he became lost in a forest and "encountered an undefinable creature," and he likened its appearance to "a fluffy cat or dog-like creature one would see in the country." Eevee has been voiced by Erica Schroeder in English and by Chinami Nishimura "Chinami Nishimura (voice actress)") in Japanese for the anime

results.title: Eevee (Pokémon) - Bulbapedia, the community-driven Pokémon encyclopedia
results.content:
Eevee (Japanese: イーブイ Eievui) is a Normal-type "Normal (type)") Pokémon "Pokémon (species)") introduced in Generation I.

It evolves into one of eight different Pokémon through various methods.

(Specifics may differ in past games. Refer to Game data→Evolution data for these details.)

Additionally, Eevee has one alternative form, the Gigantamax Eevee, which can Gigantamax into Gigantamax Eevee if it has the Gigantamax Factor. Another form, the partner "Partner Pokémon (Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!)") Eevee appears exclusively in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!, however it is not described as a unique form in the Pokédex, and is only recognized as a form in the game data. The partner Eevee and Eevee with the Gigantamax Factor cannot evolve. [...] Eevee is the game mascot of and the player's first Pokémon in Pokémon: Let's Go, Eevee!. It also serves as the player's first Pokémon in Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness and Pokémon Conquest. It also is the first Pokémon employee in Pokémon Café Mix. In Pokémon Yellow, Professor Oak had originally intended to give Eevee to the player as their first Pokémon, but it is taken by Blue "Blue (game)") instead.

## Contents

## Biology

Eevee is a small, mammalian, quadrupedal Pokémon "Pokémon (species)") with primarily brown fur. The tip of its bushy tail and its large furry collar are cream-colored. It has short, slender legs with three small toes and a pink paw pad on each foot. Eevee has brown eyes, long pointed ears with dark brown interiors, and a small black nose. [...] Eevee has an unstable and irregularly shaped genetic structure that causes it to mutate when exposed to the radiation of Evolution stones. Its DNA is easily influenced by its environment, which allows it to suddenly and perfectly adapt to harsh habitats by evolving into one of eight different Pokémon. The reason why only Eevee has such unusual genes, which are the key to solving the mysteries of Evolution, remains unknown. Even its face can end up becoming similar to that of its Trainer. Eevee is an extremely rare Pokémon mostly found in cities and towns. In Pokémon Sleep, it is suggested that it dreams about which form it will evolve into in the future.

Eevee is the only known Pokémon capable of using the Z-Move Extreme Evoboost "Extreme Evoboost (move)").

### Forms

Pokemon Database logo, with Scizor

# Eevee

Eevee is a Normal type Pokémon introduced in Generation 1.

Eevee has a Gigantamax form available in Pokémon Sword/Shield, with an exclusive G-Max move, G-Max Cuddle.

Eevee artwork by Ken Sugimori

Eevee artwork by Ken Sugimori

Additional artwork

## Pokédex data [...] | FireRed | An extremely rare POKéMON that may evolve in a number of different ways depending on stimuli. |
| LeafGreen | Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element STONES. |
| Emerald | An EEVEE has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to its environment. Radiation from various STONES causes this POKéMON to evolve. |
| Diamond Pearl | A rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by taking on different evolutionary forms. |
| Platinum | Because its genetic makeup is irregular, it quickly changes its form due to a variety of causes. |
| HeartGold | It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment. | [...] | Brilliant Diamond Shining Pearl | It is a rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by changing its appearance and capabilities when it evolves. |
| Legends: Arceus | Harbors the potential to evolve into manifold forms. Within Eevee lies the key to the mysteries of Pokémon evolution—I’m certain of it. |
| Scarlet | Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment. |
| Violet | Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones. |

results.title: Real Life Pokémon: Eevee — Paths of Evolution (Pokémon Documentary)
results.content:
Instinct and environment soon converge. Where nature crystallizes into rare stones, the imprint becomes visible: Water, Thunder, Fire, Leaf, and Ice Stones condense river flow, skyfire, embered heat, woodland growth, and winter silence. When habitat and stone align, Eevee’s form resolves—Vaporeon, Jolteon, Flareon, Leafeon, Glaceon—each an answer the world gives to the life it raised.

Not every path is mineral. Some are chosen by the heart. In daylight trust, Eevee becomes Espeon; in moonlit loyalty, Umbreon; in gentle affection that comforts and protects, Sylveon. Stones, bonds, and place: three currents that guide one small creature toward many destinies. [...] or water or night but the strength of bonds the comfort when Eevee develops a deep affection joyful overflowing when she learns to protect not only by fighting but by calming by supporting something delicate is born within her Eevee evolves into Sylveon her ribbons moving like gentle extensions of her body Sylveon is empathy made visible her ribbons sense emotions steady restless hearts and soothe her allies as an emotional support Pokemon her presence alone can feel like healing in the end Eevee is not defined by what she is meant to become but by what the world allows her to become every environment she walks through every choice she makes every bond she forms opens a new path a different future and so this small creature reminds us of something far greater than evolution itself the [...] in the silence of the morning before the world fully awakens a pair of eyes opens for the very first time small, fragile and impossibly cute this is Eevee  the mother guides her first steps offering support and encouragement her mother will ensure food and remain close during those first days she will always protect Eevee above all else she is her safe harbour until the little Eevee begins to reach what she can become within this tiny body rests an almost infinite potential still without a final shape Evee is shaped by the forces of the world and by what she lives through every step of her life tips the balance and as Eevee grows she chooses the evolution that answers her path some Eevee grow up surrounded by rivers and lakes from an early age they learn to trust the water as refuge

### Stats

### Stats

Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Having gotten even friendlier and more innocent, Eevee tries to play with anyone around, only to end up crushing them with its immense body.

Gigantamax energy upped the fluffiness of the fur around Eevee’s neck. The fur will envelop a foe, capturing its body and captivating its mind.

results.title: How to get all Eevee evolutions in Pokémon Go | Names explained | Radio Times
results.content:
Pokémon. The Pokémon Company/Nintendo/Game Freak

When Pokémon was first released, Eevee only had three evolutions: Vaporeon, Jolteon and Flareon were one of the original 151 Pokémon that debuted in Pokémon Red and Blue in 1996.

However, as the years have gone by and more games have come out, more evolutions for the popular Eevee have been released, bringing the grand total of possible evolutions up to eight(!) – more than any other Pokémon.

Each Eevee evolution is a different type, so choose carefully:

 Vaporeon (Water-type)
 Jolteon (Electric-type)
 Flareon (Fire-type)
 Umbreon (Dark-type)
 Espeon (Psychic-type)
 Leafeon (Grass-type)
 Glaceon (Ice-type)
 Sylveon (Fairy-type)

Reckon you're a Pokémon master? Pit your wits against our Pokémon quiz below! [...] A word of warning, though, getting all eight Eevee evolutions in Pokémon Go requires quite the time investment, but our help should speed things up.

It'll be well worth it, too, as Eevee and its Eeveevolutions are some of the most beloved Pokémon of all since the first three variants were introduced to the world back in Pokémon Red and Blue for the original Game Boy in 1996, the first games in the long-running series.

But as the different Eeveelutions are all different Pokémon types, we'll need different items for each. And with so much to get through, let's not dilly-dally!

## What does Eevee evolve into in Pokémon Go?

Pokémon. The Pokémon Company/Nintendo/Game Freak [...] #### Want to see this content?

This page contains content provided by Riddle. We ask for your permission before anything is loaded, as Riddle may use cookies and other technologies. To view this content, choose 'Accept and continue' to allow Riddle and its required purposes.

## How to evolve Eevee into Vaporeon, Jolteon and Flareon

Evolving Eevee in the main Pokémon games requires special items, moves and friendship levels – however, in Pokémon Go Eevee can be evolved by feeding it 25 Eevee candy.

You can get Eevee candy from catching Eevees, walking with one as a buddy Pokémon and hatching eggs. You can get more for transferring Eevees to Professor Willow!

results.title: Evolve Your Strategy with Eevee | Pokemon.com
results.content:
### Eevee

Suggested Item: Eevium Z  
 Suggested Moves: Baton Pass, Last Resort, Protect, Stored Power   
 Suggested Ability: Anticipation  
 Preferred Nature: Timid

You might expect that Eevee would need to evolve to perform well in battle, but the Evolution Pokémon could be most effective in its original form. Eevee’s unique Extreme Evoboost Z-Move increases all its stats by two levels. This effect is so powerful that some Trainers build their whole team around it! [...] Evolve Your Strategy with Eevee

# Evolve Your Strategy with Eevee

We’re celebrating Eevee this month as Trainers look forward to Pokémon: Let’s Go, Eevee!, but you don’t have to wait until November to use Eevee and its Evolutions in battle. This week, we have some great strategies for Eevee and each of its Evolutions you can try out in Pokémon Ultra Sun and Pokémon Ultra Moon.

Pokémon can excel with pretty different setups depending on whether they’re intended for Single or Double Battles. Here we’ll focus on Single Battles. Whether you’re planning to take on the competition at the Battle Tree or you’re looking for some cool team members for your next battle with your friends, these tips will help you make the most out of the Evolution Pokémon and its…uh…Evolutions.

### Eevee [...] With this strategy, you’ll want to have your Pokémon use Baton Pass in situations where you’d normally switch. Espeon is a key member of this team because its Magic Bounce Ability can block moves that would otherwise disrupt your strategy. You’ll also want to make sure your Pokémon are quicker than your foe’s so you can use Baton Pass to keep an advantageous position—make sure to include Jolteon for Agility or Eevee for Extreme Evoboost.

We hope you enjoy using your favorite Evolutions of Eevee in battle! Keep checking back to Pokémon.com as we celebrate Eevee this month, and don’t forget to check out Pokémon: Let’s Go, Pikachu! and Pokémon: Let’s Go, Eevee! on November 16, 2018.

Back to News

results.title: 8 Best Eevee Evolutions: Complete Guide (2026)
results.content:
## Let’s Play and Find the Best Eeveelution!

Now you know the best Eevee evolutions. From the formidable defensive prowess of Umbreon to the high-speed attacks of Jolteon and the powerful special attacks of Sylveon, every Eeveelution has its unique role to play in battles. The right Eeveelution can fill gaps your roster didn't know it had, so pick the one that matches your playstyle and commit.

Fans who want to celebrate all eight evolutions at once can check out the MEGA Construx Pokemon Eevee Evolution 470-Piece Building Set, which includes buildable figures of Eevee and all eight Eeveelutions. [...] #### How to Evolve

To evolve Eevee into Espeon, increase its friendship level and level it up during the daytime. To fully leverage Espeon’s potential, focus on its specialized attack and speed and utilize its Psychic-type moves to exploit the weaknesses of other Pokémon .

Shop The Pokemon Collection Now

### 5. Leafeon

Image of Leafeon

The Grass-type evolution of Eevee is Leafeon, who embodies the essence of nature with its leaf-adorned appearance and earthy tones. Known for its high attack and defense, Leafeon stands out as a powerful, nature-themed Pokémon that excels in defensive and offensive roles.

#### Key Strengths [...] ## Which Eeveelution Has the Best Stats?

Every Eeveelution has the same total base stat of 525 in mainline games, just with different stat distributions. No single Eeveelution is statistically "better" overall. Sylveon's balanced stat spread of 110 Special Attack and 130 Special Defense gives it the most versatile profile, while Umbreon's 130 Defense and 130 Special Defense make it the bulkiest. The identical totals mean the best Eeveelution depends on which stat spread best fits your team's needs. Eevee itself starts as a Normal-type with access to normal-type moves like Quick Attack, Swift, and sometimes legacy or breeding-based options like double kick or an egg move before evolving into a specialized form.

## What Eevee Evolution Is the Strongest?

results.title: How to get every Eevee evolution in Pokémon Go
results.content:
Eevee evolves using 25 Eevee candy, but if you don’t use any special tricks, the evolution you get will be random. For those looking for specific Pokémon, there are several things you can do. Players who are just looking for one of each evolution can use a simple naming trick.

## Eevee evolutions names, nicknames, and name trick

To get a specific evolution, you can name your Eevee one of the following names, and it will evolve into the corresponding variant, guaranteed.

Note: This naming trick only works once per evolution. If you already used “Sparky” to get a Jolteon, it will not guarantee that you get a Jolteon next time.

## Pokémon Go Eevee evolution names [...] Polygon.com logo
Polygon.com logo
4

Who doesn’t want a hoard of cute fox-like Pokémon?

# How to get every Eevee evolution in Pokémon Go

Eevee surrounded by its evolved siblings on a blue and green gradient background

#### Table of contents

### Jump Links

To get one of the many Eevee evolutions (or Eeveelutions, if you’re into the whole brevity thing) in Pokémon Go, you have to evolve Eevee. However, the methods for getting the right Eeveelution vary, including using certain names (or nicknames for the Eevee evolutions name trick). [...] ### How to evolve Eevee into Jolteon, Flareon, or Vaporeon

Unfortunately Jolteon, Flareon, and Vaporeonall evolve randomly from Eevee. You’ll just need to choose to evolve them using 25 Eevee Candy, but you’ll want to make sure that your Eevee has not met any of the requirements for the other Eevee evolutions (below) or else you won’t get any of these Kanto evolutions.

### How to evolve Eevee into Umbreon or Espeon

Umbreon and Espeonboth evolve after walking with the Eevee as your buddy Pokémon for 10 kilometers. Once that’s done, the buddy Eevee evolved during the night will yield Umbreon while the day will yield Espeon. Note that when you evolve Eevee, it still needs to be your buddy Pokémon for this trick to work.

### How to evolve Eevee into Leafeon or Glaceon
